children.heading: Existing Federal reclamation law
children.label: a Existing Federal reclamation law
children.text: The provisions of Federal reclamation law shall remain in full force and effect, except to the extent such law is amended by, or is inconsistent with, this subchapter.

children.heading: Existing statutory exemptions from ownership or pricing limitations of Federal reclamation law
children.label: b Existing statutory exemptions from ownership or pricing limitations of Federal reclamation law
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall repeal or amend any existing statutory exemptions from the ownership or pricing limitations of Federal reclamation law.

children.heading: Regulations; collection of necessary data
children.label: c Regulations; collection of necessary data
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations and shall collect all data necessary to carry out the provisions of this subchapter and other provisions of Federal reclamation law.

children.heading: Sale of nonexcess land acquired into excess status pursuant to involuntary process of law, etc.
children.label: e Sale of nonexcess land acquired into excess status pursuant to involuntary process of law, etc.
children.text: Any nonexcess land which is acquired into excess status pursuant to involuntary foreclosure or similar involuntary process of law, conveyance in satisfaction of a debt (including, but not limited to, a mortgage, real estate contract, or deed of trust), inheritance, or devise, may be sold at its fair market value without regard to any other provision of this subchapter or to : , That if the status of mortgaged land changes from nonexcess into excess after the mortgage is recorded and is subsequently acquired by the lender by involuntary foreclosure or similar involuntary process of law, by bona fide conveyance in satisfaction of the mortgage, such land may be sold at its fair market value.section 423e of this titleProvided

children.heading: Annual audit of compliance with reclamation laws
children.label: g Annual audit of compliance with reclamation laws
children.text: In addition to any other audit or compliance activities which may otherwise be undertaken, the Secretary of the Interior, or his designee, shall conduct a thorough audit of the compliance with the reclamation law of the United States, specifically including this subchapter, by legal entities and individuals subject to such law. At a minimum, the Secretary shall complete audits of those legal entities and individuals whose landholdings or operations exceed 960 acres within 3 years.

children.heading: Recordable contracts executed prior toOctober 12, 1982
children.label: h Recordable contracts executed prior toOctober 12, 1982
children.text: The provisions of are and have been applicable to all recordable contracts executed prior to , and any decision, rule, or regulation promulgated by the Department of the Interior to the contrary is hereby revoked: , That notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (i), the Secretary shall not seek reimbursement for any amounts due under this subsection or which was due prior to .section 390ee(c) of this titlesection 390ee(c) of this titleOctober 12, 1982December 22, 1987Provided

children.heading: Collection of underpayment with interest for irrigation water
children.label: i Collection of underpayment with interest for irrigation water
children.text: When the Secretary finds that any individual or legal entity subject to reclamation law, including this subchapter, has not paid the required amount for irrigation water delivered to a landholding pursuant to reclamation law, including this subchapter, he shall collect the amount of any underpayment with interest accruing from the date the required payment was due until paid. The interest rate shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury on the basis of the weighted average yield of all interest bearing marketable issues sold by the Treasury during the period of underpayment.
